content.claim: Short-term quasi-experimental evidence (Horn 2013, Gurzó & Horn 2015) shows early-selective academic tracks increase admitted students' 10th-grade test scores but deteriorate scores of non-admitted similar peers, while long-run labor-market effects are null
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Single research program with limited replication; long-run estimate from one working paper only
Zero long-term effect contradicts short-term value-added, creating tension in interpretation
No tertiary-education outcome data provided; study cannot confirm whether tracking fulfills preparation expectations
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
